businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margins recovered to 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model, and April 2026 brings Cybercab production start and Roadster reveal as key catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Prices in AI/robotaxi future, but overvalued on current earnings basis

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS $2.25. Still 4x+ above growth stock avg of 30-40x. Bakes in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 2025 revenue $97.8B grew just 1% YoY. 2026 expected to grow 15% to $108.9B

keyMetrics.name: Auto Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down significantly from 25%+ in 2022. Price competition and cost pressures. Q4 bounce to 20.1% signals recovery

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Larger than Toyota, VW, GM, Ford, BMW, and Hyundai combined. Valued as AI platform, not car company

keyMetrics.name: Annual Deliveries
keyMetrics.interpretation: First annual delivery decline (-1.1%) since going public. UBS expects Q1 2026 deliveries at 345K, down 18% YoY

growthDrivers.title: Oil price surge ($100/barrel) driving EV demand (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Iran war pushed Brent crude to $99.75, US gasoline up 37% to $3.98, diesel above $5.30. Reuters reports used EV sales surging in Europe. High fuel costs directly accelerate EV adoption, benefiting Tesla

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video released, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla released Optimus3 humanoid robot video showcasing reduction gearbox and dexterous hand design. Stock rose 2% pre-market. Summer 2026 production start confirms robot business is materializing

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush calls Terafab a 'game changer' (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed Outperform with $600 target, calling the Terafab semiconductor plant a 'game changer.' AI5 chip small-scale production in late 2026, volume production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: Europe sales rebound after 13 months (Feb +11.8%)
growthDrivers.description: ACEA data shows Feb European registrations up 11.8% YoY, first increase since Dec 2024. China Feb sales up 42%. BYD grew 100%+ in the same period

growthDrivers.title: April catalyst cluster: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production start, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval (4/10), and Q1 earnings all concentrated in April

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA escalates FSD investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Potential recall could delay robotaxi timeline

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B FSD miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Impossible for competitors to replicate at this scale
Supercharger NACS standard adopted by Ford, GM, and others
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging all in-house
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition drives demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China #1, 100%+ growth in Europe. Leading on price competitiveness

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas, Austin, Miami

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: 200K+ paid rides per week. Ahead on commercial autonomous driving

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding EV market share with value and quality

recentNews.headline: Iran war pushes oil near $100, gas up 37% to $3.98, boosting EV demand
recentNews.significance: Brent crude $99.75, US gasoline surged 37%. Reuters: used EV sales jumping in Europe. High fuel prices accelerate EV adoption, directly benefiting Tesla

recentNews.headline: Optimus3 robot video released, stock up 2% pre-market
recentNews.significance: Reduction gearbox and dexterous hand design revealed. Summer 2026 production start planned

recentNews.headline: Tesla's 'true believers' showing signs of losing faith
recentNews.significance: Put/call ratio hit 3-year high of 1.75. Options market signals more downside than upside risk in next 30 days

recentNews.headline: Musk announces Terafab chip factory in Austin
recentNews.significance: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture for $20-25B semiconductor plant. AI chip self-sufficiency by 2027

recentNews.headline: NHTSA escalates FSD low-visibility investigation
recentNews.significance: Safety concerns about FSD in fog and dust conditions. Potential recall or regulatory limits could delay robotaxi plans

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush's Dan Ives maintains $600 target, calling 2026 Tesla's 'transformation year.' JPMorgan at $150 warns of 'valuation disconnected from auto fundamentals.' Barclays raised to $325 on FSD progress

risks.title: Extreme valuation risk
risks.description: TTM P/E 357x is 15x the S&P 500 average. Any growth slowdown could trigger sharp decline

risks.title: FSD regulatory risk
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalation, California robotaxi denial. Regulatory barriers could significantly delay robotaxi timeline

risks.title: Core auto business stagnation
risks.description: First delivery decline, 1% revenue growth. ASP down 8%, auto margin at 16.3%. Intense competition from BYD, Xiaomi

risks.title: Musk brand risk
risks.description: DOGE politics drove European Jan sales down 45%. Insider selling ($25.6M by Kimbal Musk). Brand damage directly impacts demand

risks.title: Competition intensifying
risks.description: Uber-Rivian $1.25B robotaxi deal. Waymo at 200K+ weekly rides. Eroding Tesla's robotaxi premium

risks.title: Geopolitical risk and oil surge
risks.description: Iran war pushes oil near $100. Short-term EV demand positive but macro uncertainty pressures high-valuation stocks

buyReasons.title: Oil surge accelerates EV adoption
buyReasons.rationale: Gas at $3.98 (+37%), diesel $5.30. Europe used EV sales surging. High fuel costs make EV economics compelling

buyReasons.title: April catalyst cluster
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, Q1 earnings all in April

buyReasons.title: Energy business explosive growth
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack revenue doubled in 2025, now 12% of total. High-margin recurring revenue diversifying from auto

buyReasons.title: Technical bounce signal
buyReasons.rationale: Falling wedge and harami pattern forming. RSI 39 near oversold. 38.2% Fibonacci retracement convergence

overallOpinion: Oil prices surging 37% (gas at $3.98) from the Iran war are accelerating EV adoption. Used EV sales are jumping in Europe, providing a direct tailwind for Tesla demand
April brings a cluster of catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings. Optimus3 robot production starts this summer. The growth story is alive
However, the 357x P/E (15x S&P 500 average) remains extreme, and NHTSA's FSD probe escalation plus California's robotaxi denial create uncertainty in the autonomous timeline
Technically, falling wedge and harami patterns suggest a bounce, but the stock trades below its 50-day SMA ($405). Analyst consensus target $421 (+9%)

investmentVerdict.summary: Oil surge creates a favorable EV demand environment, but extreme valuation and regulatory uncertainty persist. Waiting for April catalyst results is the prudent approach. Long-term investors can consider $340-365 for dollar-cost averaging

threads.hook: TSLA: Oil surge is accelerating EV adoption
threads.text:
$TSLA $385.95 | $1.43T market cap

Iran war pushed gas up 37% to $3.98. Reuters reports used EV sales surging across Europe. High fuel costs are making the EV switch more compelling than ever, directly benefiting Tesla.

April brings Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings. Optimus3 starts production this summer.

But the 357x P/E, NHTSA FSD probe, and California robotaxi denial are real concerns. Falling wedge pattern suggests a bounce but 50-day SMA ($405) resistance remains.

Hold. Analyst avg $421 (+9%). Prudent to wait for April catalysts.

witty.hook: TSLA: When gas goes up, electric goes mainstream
witty.text:
$TSLA $385.95

Gas up 37% to $3.98. Suddenly EVs look genius. Europe is panic-buying used Teslas. Oil doing more for EV adoption than any government incentive ever did.

Irony: stock is down 23% from highs

1/4
thread:
April is the all-in round. Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, earnings, Optimus3.

At 357x P/E, Tesla better deliver on at least one of these or the house of cards gets wobbly

2/4

NHTSA doesn't trust FSD in fog. California said no to robotaxis. Meanwhile Wedbush sees $600.

Analyzing Tesla is an exercise in cognitive dissonance

3/4

Gas at $3.98 makes EVs a no-brainer. But does that justify 357x P/E? That's the $1.43 trillion question.

Hold. Let April answer it.

4/4

$TSLA $385.95 | $1.43T market cap

Iran war pushed gas up 37% to $3.98. Reuters reports used EV sales surging across Europe. High fuel costs are making the EV switch more compelling than ever, directly benefiting Tesla.

April brings Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings. Optimus3 starts production this summer.

But the 357x P/E, NHTSA FSD probe, and California robotaxi denial are real concerns. Falling wedge pattern suggests a bounce but 50-day SMA ($405) resistance remains.

witty.hook: Gas prices doing more for EV adoption than any government subsidy
witty.text:
$TSLA $385.95

Gas jumped 37% to $3.98. Diesel past $5.30. Suddenly everyone in Europe is buying used Teslas. Oil doing the heavy lifting for the EV transition.

Irony: Tesla stock is down 23% from its high while its demand tailwind gets stronger by the day. P/E at 357x means you're not buying a car company, you're buying a belief system.

April is packed: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, earnings, Optimus3. Five catalysts in 30 days. It's either vindication or reckoning.

Hold. Grab your popcorn and let April sort this out.

empathy.hook: TSLA investors: the tide may be turning
empathy.text:
$TSLA $385.95, down 23% from $499.

If you bought near the top, it's been a painful stretch. But here's what's changing: gas prices surged 37% to $3.98, and EV demand is responding. Used Tesla sales are jumping across Europe.

April is loaded with catalysts: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, Q1 earnings. Optimus3 production starts this summer. Any positive surprise could shift momentum.

The 357x P/E and NHTSA probe are valid concerns. But the falling wedge pattern and RSI 39 suggest we may be near a bottom.

Be patient. April will bring clarity. Analyst avg target $421 (+9%).
